blocks.text: Punishment Is Inevitable
Jeremiah Complains Again and Is Reassured
Then the Lord said to me: Though Moses and Samuel stood before me, yet my heart would not turn toward this people. Send them out of my sight, and let them go!
And when they say to you, “Where shall we go?” you shall say to them: Thus says the Lord:
And I will appoint over them four kinds of destroyers, says the Lord: the sword to kill, the dogs to drag away, and the birds of the air and the wild animals of the earth to devour and destroy.
I will make them a horror to all the kingdoms of the earth because of what King Manasseh son of Hezekiah of Judah did in Jerusalem.
Who will have pity on you, O Jerusalem,
You have rejected me, says the Lord;
I have winnowed them with a winnowing fork
Their widows became more numerous
She who bore seven has languished;
Woe is me, my mother, that you ever bore me, a man of strife and contention to the whole land! I have not lent, nor have I borrowed, yet all of them curse me.
The Lord said: Surely I have intervened in your life for good; surely I have brought enemies upon you in a time of trouble and in a time of distress.
Can one break iron, iron from the north, and bronze?
Your wealth and your treasures I will give as plunder, without price, for all your sins, throughout all your territory.
I will make you serve your enemies in a land that you do not know, for in my anger a fire is kindled that shall burn against you.
O Lord, you know;
Your words were found, and I ate them,
I did not sit in the company of merrymakers,
Why is my pain unceasing,
Therefore thus says the Lord:
And I will make you to this people
I will deliver you out of the hand of the wicked
